  instead of blanking the screen

fix: PKCE verifier — match Kit's 32-byte base64url format

Was using token_urlsafe(64) = 86 chars. Now 32 random bytes
base64url encoded = 43 chars, same as Kit. Some OIDC providers
are strict about RFC 7636 verifier length.

The CO₂ heatmap now uses the YlOrRd (yellow→orange→red) colormap

of kWh, so the same colormap produced identical-looking heatmaps.

commit.message:
feat: Data Preparation UI + Analysis Display + full 4-step flow

Data Preparation:
- MissingDataPanel: per-variable missing bars, Little's MCAR test,
  strategy selector (listwise/pairwise/mean imputation)
- RecodePanel: detection flags → confirm/dismiss → addTransform()
- PrepWorkspace: two panels + PrepLog strip with "Run Analysis" gate

Analysis Display:
- PlainLanguageCard: green interpretation card from plugin output
- MetricsRow: key numbers (mean, alpha, R², net score, sig count)
- FindingCard: significant/not-significant with p-value and effect
- StepCard: collapsible step with header, metrics, findings, chart placeholders
- AnalysisResults: renders full RunResult with completion stats
- DataTable: sortable results table

Full 4-step flow wired:
  Paste → Tag → Prepare → Analyze (→ Results)
- DataWorkspace runs HeadlessRunner with all applicable plugins
- resolveColumn() applied to all columns before analysis
- CapabilityMatcher → AnalysisRegistry.queryOrdered → plugins run
- Findings stored in FindingsStore, log entries in AnalysisLog
- Step indicator tracks progress through all 4 stages

Production build: 327KB JS (103KB gzip), 14KB CSS. 262/262 tests.

commit.message:
Fix conversion tracking: remove fallback matching, dedup, cap at 100%

- Remove aggressive fallback tiers 3 & 4 that attributed conversions to
  unrelated visitors — only match by exact session ID now
- Skip already-converted visits to prevent pixel + webhook double-counting
- Cap conversion rate display at 100% across all stats computations
- Reset 231 falsely attributed conversions in production data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: nginx passes full Host header with port ($http_host)

$host strips the port, causing callback URLs to be
http://localhost/auth/callback instead of
http://localhost:3000/auth/callback. $http_host preserves it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CI typecheck and enterprise scan: point types to src, exclude tsconfig

- Change "types" field from "dist/index.d.ts" to "src/index.ts" in 19
  workspace package.json files — dist/ doesn't exist in CI (gitignored),
  so tsc couldn't find type declarations via node_modules symlinks
- Exclude tsconfig.json from enterprise leak scan (paths mapping contains
  "core/enterprise" which is a legitimate config entry, not leaked code)
- Regenerate package-lock.json to match updated package.json files

commit.message:
Add all workspace path mappings to tsconfig for CI typecheck

tsconfig.json paths only had 6 entries but the codebase imports 23
workspace packages via @data-workers/*. Locally, TypeScript resolves
these through node_modules symlinks, but CI has no dist/ directories
so the "types": "dist/index.d.ts" fields fail. Adding all packages to
paths lets tsc resolve directly to source in both environments.

commit.message:
feat(pt_avwap): VIX-adaptive SL cap on AVWAP bracket rebuild

After AVWAP Limit→MKT conversion, cap the SL distance based on VIX:
  VIX < 20: no cap (original 1.5× ATR)
  VIX 20-25: min(1.5× ATR, 2.0% of fill)
  VIX 25-30: min(1.5× ATR, 1.5% of fill)
  VIX > 30: min(1.0× ATR, 1.0% of fill)

Updates monitor_state.sl_distance for consistent trail behavior.
Fetches VIX from FRED API (VIXCLS) at conversion time.
1054 tests passing.

feat: v4 tracks — Data Preparation, Report Generation, Session Persistence

Track 1 — Data Preparation Layer:
- missingData.ts: diagnostics, Little's MCAR test, strategy application
- computeVariable.ts: MEAN/SUM/MIN/MAX across columns, formula parser
- prepState.ts: readiness gate (readyToAnalyze = false until strategy declared)

Track 2 — Report Generation:
- ReportSchema.ts: serializable JSON document (not component tree),
  immutable add/remove/reorder, re-runnable against new data
- conditionEvaluator.ts: resolves showIf expressions (R2 > 0.3, etc.)
  evaluated before renderer sees sections — renderer never decides
- ReportRenderer.ts: interface + JSONRenderer (debug/test renderer)

Track 3 — Session Persistence:
- persistence.ts: IndexedDB setup, serialize/rehydrate all 5 stores,
  auto-save with 2s debounce, restore on app load
- sessionFile.ts: .mrst export (gzip JSON), import with schema
  version validation, browser download trigger

35 new tests: missing data diagnostics, MCAR, strategy application,
formula parsing, compute variables, condition evaluation, report
schema operations, JSON renderer, round-trip serialize/rehydrate.
262/262 total passing. Build clean.

fix(ansible): create per-user scratch dir; remove stale hosts.ini.example

- Create /mnt/lustre/<ansible_user> owned by ansible after mount so the
  sandbox user can do I/O without sudo. The Lustre root retains chmod 1777
  (shared scratch with sticky bit) for general access. Mirrors standard
  HPC per-user scratch directory pattern.

  render_inventory() which is the canonical inventory definition. The file
  added maintenance burden and would drift from actual output.

commit.message:
chore(repo): remove accidentally committed tfstate backup files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: drop dual-rail LNET, all nodes single-rail on lnet0

The dual-rail LNET configuration (tcp0 with eth1+eth2 on all server VMs)
caused persistent cascading eviction cycles in the libvirt environment.
When any lnet1 connection entered recovery, ksocklnd recovery cascaded
into lnet0, disrupting MGS keepalives from multiple servers and triggering
repeated ~150s eviction cycles that never fully resolved.

All nodes (servers and client) now use a single LNET NIC (lnet0/eth1 on
the lustre-lnet0 bridge). Single-rail is stable and appropriate for a
libvirt sandbox where both virtual NICs share the same physical host.

Changes:
- terraform/vms.tf: remove lustre_lnet1 NI from mgs, mds, and oss domains
- scripts/gen_inventory.py: remove ip_lnet1 from all server inventory lines
- ansible/group_vars/all.yml: remove mgs_lnet1_ip and lnet_iface_1;
  simplify lnet_networks to tcp0(eth1) (also fixes yamllint disable-line
  anti-pattern on that line)
- ansible/server_stg_setup.yaml: simplify mkfs.lustre --mgsnode to lnet0
  NID only for MGS, MDS, and OSS format commands
- ansible/lnet_setup.yaml: remove lustre-net1 nmcli loop item, lnetctl
  add loop item, persist task, and lnet1 ping task; all lnet1 conditionals
  removed as dead code
- ansible/templates/lustre-modules.service.j2: remove lustre-net1
  ExecStartPre line and ip_lnet1 conditional block
- ansible/host_vars/client1.yml: update comment (no longer client-specific)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Runner split — InteractiveRunner + HeadlessRunner

IStepRunner interface with runOne() and runAll().

InteractiveRunner:
- Runs one plugin at a time (NextStepButton pattern)
- Surfaces assumption violations inline — does not block
- Completes log entries with userId/fingerprint/version

HeadlessRunner (Option A — power-user shortcut):
- Batch execution, no UI interaction
- Assumption violations ALWAYS logged to AnalysisLog — never silent
- Findings flagged when preconditions violated
- Errors on individual plugins don't stop the pipeline
- Accumulates log entries for caller to write to store

11 new runner tests covering: single/batch execution, progress
callbacks, violation handling, error resilience, finding metadata.
227/227 total passing. Build clean.

commit.message:
fix: seed-synthetic — generate numeric Google IDs to match \d{7,13} filter

Synthetic accounts used XXX-XXX-XXXX format (with dashes) which was
excluded by the listAccounts regex filter, causing 0 accounts in the
accounts tab while dashboard ban_logs still showed them.

Now generates 10-digit numeric IDs matching real Google Ads CID format.
Existing seeded data with dashed IDs should be cleared:
  DELETE FROM accounts WHERE google_account_id ~ '^\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}$';
